MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PES-06-289350 The Estate of Marshall I. Wais and PTR-08-291579 the Marshall I. Wais Trust.

Appearances: Andrew Zabronsky for Robert L. Farmer. Stacie Nelson and Nicole Neubert for Lonna Wais. Walter Cook for and with Judge Elaine Rushing (Trustee ad Litem). Richard Collier for (remaindermen) Marshall Wais Jr., Peter Wais, Michael Wais and Laura Wais (also present).

Mr. Collier reported that Ms. Wais and the 4 remaindermen reached a settlement, that it has been documented and signed with terms including the trust to be terminated, IRS procedures regarding marital dissolution being followed, release of all claims in the 850 petition, discharge of the Trustee ad Litem and that they would be meeting on Monday to iron out the last details. He also stated that they intend to file a petition for approval of the settlement agreement by mid October and would like implementation by the end of the year. Ms. Nelson reported that settlement with Mr. Weaver and the Farmers estate is still in the works, the remaindermen have agreed to waive notice, are in approval of the accountings in the trust case, that the objections of Mrs. Wais are being resolved with the settlement and that the Unitrust petition is to be dismissed. In addition, Mr. Collier clarified that all of this activity is in the trust matter, not the estate and that once the issues are resolved they will settle the estate. All parties expressed desire to have the petition for approval of settlement heard as early as possible. The court advised counsel that the earlier the petition gets filed the better and signed the stipulation for order staying discovery and tolling the 5 year statute for bringing the amended 850 petition to trial. Status on administration of estate was set for November 18, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. The petition for removal in the trust matter is also continued to that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Court Reporter: Wendy Wachhorst, CSR #12290, 650-208-0405 Bailiff: Deputy R. McConoco. (Reported)

MINI MINUTES: Heard with case number PTR-08-291579, Wais Trust

Appearances: William Norman for the Wais children. Claudia Lauder for Robert Farmer (Trustee). Walter Cook for and with Elaine Rushing. Mark Davey for and with Lonna Wais.

Mr. Cook introduced to the Court the Honorable Judge Elaine Rushing (Trustee Ad Litim), stated that he was asking the Court to amend the 850 petition to substitute Judge Rushing as the Petitioner and presented the Court with an ex parte application and order for the same signed by all counsel. The Court granted Mr. Cook's request (subject to filing of the ex parte petition and review of the order). Mr. Norman pointed out that the petition to convert to unitrust was not verified and argued that there is no showing at all for the need to invade principal and that it is contrary to the decedent's wishes. Ms. Lauder advised the Court that Mr. Matiasevich will not be available until mid June. Counsel was asked to step out of the court room to meet and confer and to develop a plan as to how they want to proceed with regard to exchange of documents, depositions, and the five year statute with regard to taking the matters to trial, etc. Upon returning, counsel presented a plan of action, the Court stated that it does not see how it can entertain the petition to convert trust to a unitrust when it does not know what is in the trust and that it did not think that they were ready to go to trial. Counsel confirmed that the trial on the 850 petition will be more than 2 half days and will need to be sent out to the Department of the Presiding Judge. The Court raised the issue of consolidation. All matters were continued to May 29, 2013 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept 204 to see where the parties are with regard to discovery and trial setting. The issue of converting the trust to a unitrust is bifurcated from the petition to surcharge trustee and is set for a one afternoon hearing on July 25, 2013 at 1:30 p.m., in Dept 204.

Appearances: Matthew Matiasevich for Robert Farmer. William Norman for Remaindermen. Mark Davey and Neil Horton for Lonna Wais (Income Beneficiary).

The Court stated that it still thinks this is a dispute between Lonna and the Children, provided thoughts on how they might proceed, stated that there are 850 Petitions in both matters which list all of the 30 properties, that it's thinking is to deny both Petitions for Instructions as a matter of law on the basis that is not the type of question the Court should instruct upon, lift the stay on both of the 850 Petitions which brings all of the issues before the Court, substitute the Children in on the 850 Petitions as Petitioners and that the Trustee can remain neutral. Counsel presented their views/concerns. Counsel discussed the issues raised by the Court outside of the courtroom and upon returning agreed to the following: 1) All pleadings with respect to the 850 Petitions will be filed in the Trust matter (291579). 2) Mr. Horton will file a Motion for Judgment on the Pleadings in both matters by September 18, 2012 with hearing dates of November 1, 2012 at 2:00 p.m. 3) Mr. Matiasevich will file a brief addressing the issues raised by the Court as stated above with regard to lifting the 850 Petitions and denying the Petition for Instructions by September 18, 2012. 4) Mr. Matiasevich and Mr. Norman will file oppositions to the Motions for Judgment on the Pleadings by Ocotber 9, 2012. 5) Mr. Horton and Mr. Norman will file responses to Mr. Matiasevich's brief by October 9, 2012. 6) Replies to the the motions if any and Mr. Matiasevich's reply to the issue on the Petition for Instructions are to be filed by October 23, 2012. Both Petitions for Instructions were continued to November 6, 2012 at 10:30 a.m. (special set), in Dept. 204. On October 10, 2012, the Court will issue an order lifting the stay on both of the 850 Petitions.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Court Reporter: Tammy Willat-Harrison, CSR# 4609. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Santiago.

MINI MINUTES: This matter was heard with case number 291579 (The Marshall I. Wais Trust).

Appearances: Christina Nelson and Richard Greene making a limited appearance for the Wais Children, (Children of the Decedent). Peter Moye for Lonna Wais (Surviving Spouse). Matthew Matiasevich for Robert Farmer.

Mr. Greene informed the Court that the order signed two weeks ago was being appealed and requested that the two responses/replies filed by his Clients be withdrawn in order to not jeopardize them. At the request of Counsel, and there being no objections by Mr. Moye or Mr. Matiasevich, the Court will sign an order to withdraw the following documents: Reply In Support Of Trustee's Petition For Instructions Authorizing Disclosure Of Requested Information filed in case number 291579 on February 13, 2009 and the Reply In Support Of Executor's Disclosure Petition filed in case number 289350 on February 13, 2009. Ms. Nelson will prepare the order. Mr. Matiasevich stated that he had nothing to add, that nothing had been filed and stated that an order should be issued with regard to the conflicting demands placed on his Client. Mr. Moye stated that there was a 15 year relationship with the Client, that it was premature to turn over the documents and that it should not be done until a neutral party was in place. MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Richard Greene for the Petitioner's in the Safe Harbor Petition. Peter Moye for Lonna Wais. Mathew Matiasevich for Robert Farmer (Trustee).

The Court stated that it had reviewed all the filed documents, that it was only dealing with the two issues before it today, and that it would not hear argument on the underlying petitions. Mr. Greene stated that they were trying to ascertain what the decedent was trying to do and that he did not have much to add to his moving papers. Mr. Moye stated that the Court had narrowed it down to the issue of separate vs. community. The Court stated that it did not believe that the documents attached to the two petitions violated the no contest clause and that it would issue an order (the matter was submitted). Discussions were held regarding how the Court would proceed on the remaining petitions. The Court stated that the Petition for Instruction would be heard on February 25, 2009, the hearing on the Transfer of Property would also be heard on February 25, 2009, but it would not rule on the Petition for Removal. In addition, the Court stated that it assumed replies would be filed and if an evidentiary hearing needed to be set it would set one.

Judge: David L. Ballati. Reporter: Susan Lee, CSR#4280. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy Sandberg.
